notes: 12 available. OUT: Matt Watson, Perry DiLibero #31, Corey Cheever, Kyle Doherty, Luke McArdle. Our best 3B (Watson) and best LF (DiLibero) are both out, so Ed Fitzpatrick anchors 3B all four innings Klatt pitches (then slides to 2B innings 5-7 when Klatt takes 3B) - Ed plays all 7. Thacher Knight is the everyday LF. Andrew Amann plays all 7 at LCF. Jon Sorenson catches innings 1/5/6, spells 1B innings 2/4, and sits inn 3 & 7. Klatt & Joe McLean split the 7 innings 4/3 (Klatt 1-4, Joe 5-7). Catcher rotates Sorenson (1/5/6), Coulombe (2/4/7) and Konen (3) - nobody catches all game, and Coulombe also gets OF innings.
sits_note: Twelve bodies, ten gloves, so two lucky souls get a bench cameo every inning - consider it a mandatory spa day, no politics, no grudges, just arithmetic doing arithmetic things. Three iron men never so much as graze the pine: Marks (the only shortstop that exists on this roster - if he sits, SS becomes a rumor), Amann (LCF is his legal residence tonight) and Ed Fitzpatrick (with Watson out, the hot corner doesn't babysit itself). Everybody else takes a number. Sorenson sits twice (inn 3 & 7) - catcher's gear is a personal sauna, and now that Ed hoards third base, Jon's old 3B side-gig evaporated. The right-field committee - Coulombe, Konen, Bruno - plus Sang at first each rest twice, because those spots are stacked three-deep and the math has options. Joe, Knight and Flinn get one breather apiece. Nobody sits three times, nobody sits back-to-back (we're generous, not cruel), and everyone bats all game regardless - SIT means a fielding nap, not a benching. Play clean defense, sit less. It's basically a redemption arc with box scores.

opponent_lineup.source: 2025 scouting (65 PA on file): a pull/left-heavy, right-handed attack - 24 balls in play to the LEFT side vs 13 to the right, with center mostly to the outfield. Shade the defense left / left-center and make them beat you the other way.

rules: VALIDATION RULE (a.k.a. the part where the math pinky-promises not to embarrass us): every inning fields exactly ten humans across the ten spots - P, C, 1B, 2B, 3B, SS, LF, LCF, RCF, RF - one glove per position, zero abandoned patches of grass, and zero instances of Ed existing in two places at once (a backtracking solver double-checked all seven innings like a hall monitor with a laminated clipboard). Now, the deep lore behind tonight's calls: Watson AND DiLibero both RSVP'd 'no', which robbed us of our best third baseman and our best left fielder in a single evening - rude. So Ed Fitzpatrick adopts the hot corner for all four innings Klatt pitches (Klatt can't pitch and play third simultaneously, see the 'no cloning' clause above), then slides to 2B once Klatt inherits third base in innings 5-7; Ed plays all 7 because somebody had to, and he volunteered by the ancient method of Being Reliable. Knight is your everyday LF, chosen by the rigorous process of 'who's left.' Marks is the only shortstop in the known universe, so he goes wire-to-wire whether his hamstrings consent or not. Amann owns LCF for all 7. Klatt and Joe McLean split the mound 4/3 like a very civil custody arrangement, and the catcher's mask rotates through Sorenson, Coulombe and Konen so nobody's knees file paperwork. Twelve players, ten spots, two rest each inning - the grid tells you where to stand; the solver swears on its life it's all legal.
Pitching split 4/3: Eric Klatt pitches innings 1-4, Joe McLean pitches innings 5-7.
Dennis Marks at SS all 7 (only shortstop). Andrew Amann at LCF all 7.
Watson & DiLibero out: Ed Fitzpatrick is the everyday 3B (3B innings 1-4, then 2B innings 5-7 when Klatt takes the corner) - plays all 7; Knight is the everyday LF.
Catcher rotates among Sorenson, Coulombe and Konen - nobody catches the whole game; Coulombe also plays right-side OF.
Batting order = on-base/AVG at the top (Sang, Klatt), the run producers clustered 3-4-5 (Amann, Marks, Joe), hottest remaining bats after.

grid.why: Leadoff: team-best .714 AVG/OBP, all contact/singles - pure table-setter. 1B (sits inn 2 & 4).
Two-hole: .650 AVG, 12 singles, elite contact. Pitches innings 1-4, then mans 3B innings 5-7 (plays all 7).
Three-hole: best all-around bat (2.294 OPS, .706 AVG, 4 HR, 13 RBI). LCF all 7.
Cleanup: team-high 15 RBI and 4 HR. Only shortstop - anchors SS all 7.
Five-hole: 8 RBI. Mans 2B innings 1-3, then pitches innings 5-7. Sits inn 4.
2.256 OPS - 2nd-best bat on the team. Best 1B, but works the OF tonight (Sorenson spells 1B). Sits inn 2 & 6.
1.286 OPS, 6 RBI. Right-side OF; catches inn 3. Sits inn 1 & 5.
1.085 OPS. Everyday LF with DiLibero out (covers 2B inn 4). Sits inn 7.
.917 OPS. Right-side OF all night. Sits inn 3 & 6.
.905 OPS. Catches inn 2/4/7 and plays OF inn 3/6 - not behind the plate all game. Sits inn 1 & 5.
Catches inn 1/5/6 and spells 1B inn 2/4. Sits inn 3 & 7.
Everyday 3B with Watson out - mans the hot corner all four innings Klatt pitches, then 2B innings 5-7. Plays all 7.
